sections.title: Foundational Synthesis — Platform Classification & Security Stack Position
required_elements: Security domain classification (endpoint/EDR/XDR, network/firewall/SASE, cloud security/CNAPP/CSPM, identity/IAM/zero trust, SIEM/SOAR, email security, exposure management)
Single-domain vs. multi-domain platform positioning assessment
Module adoption rate tracking (% of customers using 2+, 4+, 6+ modules)
Leadership background (technical CISO/engineering vs. go-to-market) credibility assessment
Insider alignment analysis (insider ownership %, SBC as % of revenue, insider transaction patterns)
M&A strategy tracking (tuck-in vs. transformational, acquisition multiples, integration success)

sections.title: Unit Economics & Revenue Engine — The Security Platform Monetization Machine
required_elements: ARR and net new ARR tracking with 8-quarter trending
NRR decomposition (gross retention + expansion) with benchmark comparison
Module adoption rate by customer segment with adoption acceleration tracking
Customer concentration by segment (enterprise, mid-market, SMB revenue %)
CAC payback period vs. sector median (18-24 months target)
Magic Number calculation (net new ARR / prior-quarter S&M spend)
LTV/CAC ratio analysis (>3x healthy, <2x warning signal)
Sales channel breakdown (direct vs. channel vs. marketplace)
Gross margin and subscription gross margin analysis (75-80%+ target)
Operating margin trajectory and steady-state target assessment
SBC as % of revenue and FCF margin vs. GAAP operating margin divergence

sections.title: Competitive & Ecosystem Mapping
required_elements: Competitive win rate assessment vs. primary competitors
Competitive positioning basis (detection efficacy, price, distribution, platform breadth)
Third-party evaluation positioning (MITRE ATT&CK, Gartner MQ, Forrester Wave, SE Labs, AV-TEST)
Analyst positioning vs. market share divergence analysis
Vendor consolidation dynamics (is company a consolidator or consolidation target?)
Platform breadth score (credible coverage across core security domains)
Vendor consolidation metric (customers reducing other security vendors when adopting)
AI strategy assessment (autonomous SOC, AI analyst, detection model architecture, revenue vs. cost optimization)
AI as revenue driver vs. cost optimizer distinction
Cloud marketplace exposure (AWS, Azure, GCP marketplace % of new business)
Microsoft competitive risk and coexistence/displacement strategy

sections.title: Peer Comparison & Competitive Benchmarking
required_elements: Peer selection (2-3 direct competitors as valuation benchmark)
Financial metric comparison (revenue, ARR, growth, margin, NRR, Magic Number, customer count)
Valuation multiple comparison (EV/Revenue, EV/ARR, EV/Gross Profit, P/S, EV/FCF)
Valuation gap analysis (premium/discount vs. peers justified?)
Implied growth rate analysis (market pricing in specific growth expectations)
Qualitative competitive edge assessment (product, platform, GTM, AI, financial durability)
Relative ranking on key dimensions (1-5 scale)

sections.title: Financial Logic & Valuation
required_elements: Valuation framework by growth stage (high-growth >30%: EV/Revenue; transition 20-30%: EV/Gross Profit; mature <20%: EV/FCF)
Scenario matrix (bull, base, bear cases with specific assumptions)
Path to profitability / margin expansion modeling with opex leverage assumptions
Rule of 40 optimization timeline

sections.title: Pattern Matching & IDP Flagging
required_elements: ARR growth vs. billings growth divergence (sales cycle elongation leading indicator)
NRR declining while module count increases (module bundling at lower prices)
Gross retention stable but NRR compressing (lack of cross-sell)
SBC % of revenue increasing while growth decelerates (dilution signal)
MITRE ATT&CK scores improving but win rates flat (product vs. GTM problem)
Customer count growing but net new ARR declining (mix shift to SMB)
Capex/R&D for AI without new AI revenue SKU (cost sink signal)
FCF positive GAAP but SBC exceeds FCF (technically cash-burning)

sections.title: Investigation Tracks
required_elements: CISO channel checks and win/loss analysis with specific survey sources (Piper Sandler, Morgan Stanley)
Detection efficacy validation with third-party testing data (MITRE, SE Labs, AV-TEST, Tolly)
Platform adoption and expansion verification through module adoption cohort data

structural_requirements: All sections present with markdown headings
Security domain classification clearly stated (single vs. multi-domain platform positioning)
Module adoption rate documented with percentage of customers in 2+, 4+, 6+ module categories
Leadership credibility assessed (technical background, platform transition navigation history)
Insider alignment quantified (insider ownership %, SBC % of revenue, net insider transaction direction)
ARR, net new ARR, and 8-quarter trending documented with growth deceleration assessment
NRR decomposed into gross retention and expansion with peer benchmark comparison
CAC payback period calculated and compared to sector median
Magic Number and LTV/CAC ratio computed with health assessment
Customer concentration quantified (% from enterprise, mid-market, SMB segments)
Gross margin and subscription gross margin calculated with trajectory
SBC as % of revenue tracked with divergence from GAAP operating margin noted
Competitive win rates vs. specific competitors documented
MITRE ATT&CK, Gartner, and Forrester positioning noted with analyst consensus assessment
Platform breadth scored (% coverage of core security domains)
Vendor consolidation metrics documented (% of customers using multiple modules, vendor reduction intent)
AI strategy differentiated between revenue-driving and cost-optimization initiatives
Microsoft Defender/Sentinel displacement risk assessed
Peer comparison table constructed (minimum 2-3 peers, 10+ metrics)
Valuation gap analysis identifying justification or mispricing
Scenario analysis presents bull, base, bear cases with specific NRR, module adoption, and win rate assumptions
Investigation tracks include specific CISO survey sources, third-party testing sources, and module adoption data requirements
